fact: February 10 is exactly 41 days into the year, which means you've already lived through more than 11% of the entire year!
In the Northern Hemisphere, groundhogs are typically still fast asleep underground on this date, dreaming through their winter hibernation with their heart rate slowed to just 5 beats per minute.
In many northern places, the air often reaches some of its lowest humidity levels of the entire year around February 10, which is why your fingers might feel extra dry!
February 10 falls in the middle of Aquarius season, making it one of the most 'watery' days of the year according to astrology, even though it's winter in many places.

Maple trees in cold regions are secretly getting ready - their sap is usually starting to flow upward from their roots around this time, preparing for the sweet maple syrup season that's just weeks away.
In Antarctica, February 10 typically brings some of the warmest temperatures of their summer, when penguins are molting and look hilariously scruffy with feathers falling off everywhere.
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around February 10 in most northern places, meaning you gain 14 extra minutes of daylight just in one week!
February 10 sits exactly 39 days before the spring equinox, making it the perfect 'countdown to spring' day when winter starts feeling less endless.
Owls in many regions are at their noisiest around this date, hooting up a storm during their mating season when they're trying to impress potential partners with their vocal skills.
In places with snow, February 10 often marks when snow crystals start changing shape due to temperature fluctuations, creating those satisfying crunchy footsteps instead of soft powder.
When there's a full moon on February 10, it creates the 'Snow Moon' - often the brightest and most reflective full moon because snow acts like a giant mirror.
Earthworms in warmer climates typically start wiggling closer to the surface around this time, getting ready for spring even though there might still be weeks of cold weather ahead.
